---
title: "Site Map"
author: "Tessa Rehill"
date: "2024-12-03"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

#read libraries 
```{r}
library(tidyr)
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)
library(here)
library(maps)
library(dplyr)
library(ggmap)
library(maps)
library(ggspatial)

```

#read data 
```{r}
#read data 
sites <- read.csv("/Users/tessarehill/Dropbox/MSc/Exclosures/raw_data/metadata/site_coordinates.csv")
```

#use ggmaps to create a map of the study area 
```{r}
#register API to use ggmaps
register_google('AIzaSyBG4StmTBuRubdtKeUZWPt-1E1F08uWc-U') #register API 

register_stadiamaps("6d8a12a4-632a-40eb-8730-e997a9e3f9ed")
```

#create a baseline map for Canada (as an inset)
```{r}
#map of Canada
map.canada <- map_data("world", region = "Canada")

p1 <- ggplot(map.canada, aes(x = long, y = lat, group = group))
p1 <- p1 + geom_polygon(fill = "grey", color = "white") # Set fill to white, color to grey, and fill.alpha to 1
p1 <- p1 + theme_void() # Use a void theme for a clean background


p1 #display the map of Canada

ggsave("/Users/tessarehill/Dropbox/MSc/Exclosures/plots/maps/canada.png", 
       plot = p1,
       width = 5, height = 5, units = "in")
```

#creating map of Vancouver Island 
```{r}
#changing colors 
map.for.samples <- get_stadiamap(
  bbox = c(left = -128, bottom = 48, right = -123, top = 51), # Bounding box
  maptype = "stamen_toner_background",                        # Map type
  zoom = 8                                                    # Zoom level (adjust as needed)
)

ggmap::ggmap(map.for.samples)

#invert colors in raster
invert <- function(x) rgb(t(255-col2rgb(x))/255)    
m_inv <- as.raster(apply(map.for.samples, 2, invert))

#copy attributes from original object
class(m_inv) <- class(map.for.samples)
attr(m_inv, "bb") <- attr(map.for.samples, "bb")

ggmap(m_inv)

map.for.samples <- m_inv

#change opacity of basemap, to match Canada inset 
mapatt <- attributes(map.for.samples)
map_transparent <- matrix(adjustcolor(map.for.samples, alpha.f = 0.2), nrow = nrow(map.for.samples))
attributes(map_transparent) <- mapatt

#changing colors and removing grid lines
ggmap(map_transparent) +
  theme_void()  # This removes grid lines and other background elements

p2 <- ggmap(map_transparent) +
  theme_void()  # This removes grid lines and other background elements

ggsave("/Users/tessarehill/Dropbox/MSc/Exclosures/plots/maps/vancouver_island.png", 
       plot = p2,
       width = 5, height = 5, units = "in")

```


#creating map of sites (in Barkley sound )
```{r}
#changing colors 
map.for.samples <- get_stadiamap(
  bbox = c(left = -125.4, bottom = 48.75, right = -124.75, top = 49), # Bounding box
  maptype = "stamen_toner_background",                        # Map type
  zoom = 11                                                    # Zoom level (adjust as needed)
)

ggmap::ggmap(map.for.samples)

# invert colors in raster
invert <- function(x) rgb(t(255-col2rgb(x))/255)    
m_inv <- as.raster(apply(map.for.samples, 2, invert))

#copy attributes from original object
class(m_inv) <- class(map.for.samples)
attr(m_inv, "bb") <- attr(map.for.samples, "bb")

ggmap(m_inv)

map.for.samples <- m_inv

#change opacity of basemap, to match Canada inset 
mapatt <- attributes(map.for.samples)
map_transparent <- matrix(adjustcolor(map.for.samples, alpha.f = 0.2), nrow = nrow(map.for.samples))
attributes(map_transparent) <- mapatt


map.for.samples <- ggmap(map_transparent) + 
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank(), panel.background = element_rect(fill = 'white'))+
  geom_text(data = sites,
            aes(x = lon_decimal, y = lat_decimal, label = site, fontface = "bold"),
            size = 5, position = position_jitter(width = 0.045, height = 0.045)) +
  ylab("latitude") + xlab("longitude") +
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank(),
        panel.background = element_rect(fill = 'white'),
        axis.text = element_text(size = 10))  # Adjust the size of axis text

map.for.samples

#define density colors 
density_colors <- c('low' = '#4da607', 'medium' = '#f9cd44', 'high' = '#cd3737')

sites$density <- factor(sites$density, levels = c("high", "medium", "low"))

#modify your ggmap plot
map.for.samples <- ggmap(map_transparent) + 
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank(), panel.background = element_rect(fill = 'white')) +
  geom_point(data = sites, 
             aes(x = lon_decimal, y = lat_decimal, color = density), 
             size = 3) +  # Adjust size of the points
  scale_color_manual(values = density_colors) +  # Apply color mapping
  geom_text(data = sites, 
            aes(x = lon_decimal, y = lat_decimal, label = site), 
            size = 3,  # Adjust the size of the labels
            #position = position_jitter(width = 0.0045, height = 0.0045),  # Only apply jitter to labels
            hjust = -0.06, # Adjust the vertical position of the labels
            vjust = -0.03,
            fontface = "bold", color = "black") +  # Label styling
  ylab("Latitude") + xlab("Longitude") +
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank(),
        panel.background = element_rect(fill = 'white'),
        axis.text = element_text(size = 10))  # Adjust the size of axis text

#display the map
map.for.samples

p3 <- map.for.samples

ggsave("/Users/tessarehill/Dropbox/MSc/Exclosures/plots/maps/sites.png", 
       plot = p3,
       width = 7, height = 6, units = "in")



```
#Adding symbology and north arrow 
```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(ggmap)
library(ggspatial)

# Define colors and shapes for density levels
density_colors <- c('low' = '#4da607', 'medium' = '#f9cd44', 'high' = '#cd3737')
density_shapes <- c('low' = 15, 'medium' = 16, 'high' = 17)  # Square, Circle, Triangle

# Ensure 'density' is a factor with correct level order
sites$density <- factor(sites$density, levels = c("high", "medium", "low"))

# Modify ggmap plot
map.for.samples <- ggmap(map_transparent) + 
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank(), 
        panel.background = element_rect(fill = 'white')) +
  
  # Plot points with different colors and shapes
  geom_point(data = sites, 
             aes(x = lon_decimal, y = lat_decimal, color = density, shape = density), 
             size = 3) +  
  
  # Apply color and shape mapping
  scale_color_manual(values = density_colors) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = density_shapes) +
  
  # Add site labels
  geom_text(data = sites,
            aes(x = lon_decimal, y = lat_decimal, label = site),
            size = 3, fontface = "bold", color = "black",
            hjust = -0.06, vjust = -0.03) +
  
  # Add north arrow
  annotation_north_arrow(location = "topleft", 
                         which_north = "true", 
                         style = north_arrow_fancy_orienteering) +

  # Axis labels and formatting
  ylab("Latitude") + xlab("Longitude") + 
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank(),
        panel.background = element_rect(fill = 'white'),
        axis.text = element_text(size = 10))  

# Display the map
map.for.samples

# Save the plot
ggsave("/Users/tessarehill/Dropbox/MSc/Exclosures/plots/maps/sites.png",
       plot = map.for.samples, width = 7, height = 6, units = "in")

map.for.samples + 

```
#troubleshooting
```{r}


# Ensure your map data has a CRS
map_transparent <- st_as_sf(map_transparent, coords = c("lon", "lat"), crs = 4326)

# Modify ggmap plot
map.for.samples <- ggmap(map_transparent) + 
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank(), 
        panel.background = element_rect(fill = 'white')) +
  
  # Plot points with different colors and shapes
  geom_point(data = sites, 
             aes(x = lon_decimal, y = lat_decimal, color = density, shape = density), 
             size = 3) +  
  
  # Apply color and shape mapping
  scale_color_manual(values = density_colors) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = density_shapes) +
  
  # Add site labels
  geom_text(data = sites,
            aes(x = lon_decimal, y = lat_decimal, label = site),
            size = 3, fontface = "bold", color = "black",
            hjust = -0.06, vjust = -0.03) +
  
  # Add north arrow (ensure CRS is defined)
  annotation_north_arrow(location = "topleft", 
                         which_north = "true", 
                         style = north_arrow_fancy_orienteering) +

  # Convert to coord_sf() for ggspatial to work
  coord_sf(crs = 4326) +  # Ensure CRS is explicitly set for proper projection
  
  # Add scale bar
  annotation_scale(location = "bottomright",  # Adjust position as needed
                   bar_cols = c("black", "white"),
                   line_width = 1) +
  
  # Axis labels and formatting
  ylab("Latitude") + xlab("Longitude") + 
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank(),
        panel.background = element_rect(fill = 'white'),
        axis.text = element_text(size = 10))   

# Display the map
map.for.samples

```
